content: Canonical schema: tenants, sessions, skills, rate-limits, cost-tracking, audit. All use TENANT#{id} as PK. See Chimera-Final-Architecture-Plan.md

title: Project codename: AWS Chimera
rationale: Multi-formed Greek creature reflects multi-tenant, multi-agent, multi-platform, self-evolving nature. AWS internal codename style (Bedrock, Mantle, Kuiper). CLI: chimera deploy/agent/skill.

content: Multi-tenant isolation enforcement: Never rely on FM reasoning for authorization decisions (prompt injection vulnerability). Use deterministic enforcement: IAM policies, Cedar policies, DynamoDB condition expressions, S3 bucket policies. AgentCore Memory namespace must be validated at runtime with assertions.

name: multi-table-dynamodb-design
description: DynamoDB schema design for multi-tenant SaaS: Use 6-table design over single-table when different concerns need different TTL policies, encryption requirements, and access patterns. Enhanced tenant config uses multi-item pattern (TENANT#{id} with SK=PROFILE|CONFIG#features|CONFIG#models) for atomic config updates and flexible schema evolution.
GSI cross-tenant data leakage: Global secondary indexes on shared DynamoDB tables can leak data across tenants if not filtered at application layer.
resolution: Add FilterExpression='tenantId = :tid' to all GSI queries. Document in validation report with code examples. Add integration test to verify GSI isolation.

content: AgentCore Runtime sessions are ephemeral with no persistent filesystem. Use S3 for agent workspace persistence, not EFS. Files written during a session are lost on termination unless explicitly saved to S3.

AgentCore Memory namespace template: 'tenant-{tenant_id}-user-{user_id}' for cross-tenant isolation. Memory content is stored in AgentCore managed service, not DynamoDB. DynamoDB stores memory_id and namespace_template only.

name: multi-tenant-endpoint-isolation
description: Pool/Hybrid tiers share a single AgentCore Runtime endpoint ARN. Premium/Silo tier gets dedicated endpoint ARN per tenant. Map tenant_id → endpoint_arn in DynamoDB tenants table for routing decisions.

title: AgentCore Memory strategy tier mapping
rationale: Cost optimization: Basic tier uses only SUMMARY strategy (~/tenant/mo), Advanced adds USER_PREFERENCE (~/tenant/mo), Premium uses full suite including SEMANTIC + EPISODIC (~/tenant/mo). Balances capability vs cost per tier.

content: 8-stack CDK architecture: Network, Data, Security, Observability, PlatformRuntime, Chat, Tenant, Pipeline. Each stack has single responsibility, enabling independent deployment and blast radius control.

AgentCore MicroVMs are ephemeral — no EFS mount support. For POSIX workspace needs, use Fargate sidecar MCP tools with EFS mounted. Pattern: AgentCore for compute, Fargate sidecars for stateful services. S3 is the primary durable storage for agent workspaces.

title: 6-table DDB schema for multi-tenant SaaS
rationale: Per-tenant IAM isolation via LeadingKeys works better with separate tables, independent scaling/TTL/encryption per table, clearer separation of concerns. Enhanced tenant config uses multi-item pattern.

title: 8-stack CDK architecture
rationale: Separation of concerns, independent deployment, team ownership, blast radius control, compliance boundaries. Current 3/8 is Phase 0.

name: GSI cross-tenant leakage prevention
description: GSI queries on shared DynamoDB tables can leak cross-tenant data. Always add FilterExpression with tenantId on GSI queries for skills and audit tables. IAM LeadingKeys condition only protects PK-based queries, not GSI scans.

Merge conflict resolved at tier ai-resolve. Branch: overstory/builder-help-completions/chimera-5c0f. Agent: builder-help-completions. Conflicting files: packages/cli/src/cli.ts, packages/cli/src/commands/completion.ts.

title: AWS Services Integration Priority
rationale: Bedrock enables multi-provider LLM support. KMS provides per-tenant encryption. WAF/CloudFront deliver security and global performance. ElastiCache and Aurora address data layer gaps.

name: self-modifying-iac-dynamodb-cdk
description: DynamoDB-driven CDK synthesis enables agent-autonomous infrastructure. Pattern: DynamoDB tenant config → Streams → Lambda synthesis → CodePipeline auto-deploy. Eliminates manual CDK edits for tenant onboarding.

content: L3 construct pattern for multi-tenant isolation: TenantAgent construct encapsulates 15+ resources with built-in security (DynamoDB partition DENY, S3 prefix scoping, tier-based model access). 318 lines replace 600+ lines of HCL. Enables rapid tenant provisioning with security defaults.

title: Continue AWS CDK for IaC (reject OpenTofu/Pulumi)
rationale: Type safety prevents multi-tenant errors, L3 constructs reduce boilerplate 60%, AWS-native Bedrock support same-day, self-modifying patterns achievable via DynamoDB-driven synthesis. Migration cost 6 weeks unjustified.

content: A2A vs MCP protocols are complementary, not competing: MCP connects single agent to tools/data (vertical integration), A2A connects multiple agents to each other (horizontal coordination). Example: retail agent uses MCP for inventory DB queries, then A2A to coordinate with supplier agent for ordering.

name: aws-messaging-service-selection
description: SQS for task queuing with retries (pull-based), SNS for fan-out notifications (push-based), EventBridge for content-based routing and cross-account. Combine for complex workflows (EventBridge → SNS → SQS).

name: multi-agent-shared-memory
description: ElastiCache/Redis for sub-ms session state and distributed locks, DynamoDB for persistent state with strong consistency, S3 for large artifacts, EFS for shared filesystem. Choose based on latency and persistence needs.

name: skeleton-first-research
description: Create files with frontmatter and section headers before content fills, enabling parallel work and clear structure

content: Evolution research covers two dimensions: (1) self-modifying infrastructure with Cedar policy constraints, GitOps workflow, cost controls; (2) ML-driven evolution with auto-skill generation, prompt A/B testing, Bayesian model routing, Bedrock fine-tuning

Canonical DynamoDB schema documentation pattern: Single authoritative document superseding multiple conflicting designs. Use frontmatter with version, status=canonical, supersedes list, and authority statement. Include migration path for breaking changes.

name: enhanced-multi-item-tenant-config
description: DynamoDB tenant config using multiple items (SK=PROFILE, CONFIG#features, CONFIG#models, BILLING#current, QUOTA#{resource}) instead of single SK=META item. Enables atomic updates to config sections, flexible schema evolution, and fine-grained IAM permissions. Migration: dual-write → dual-read with fallback → background migration → deprecate old pattern.

title: SSE bridge as standalone package
rationale: @chimera/sse-bridge provides clean separation between Strands agent runtime and Vercel AI SDK frontends. Supports both Node.js streams (Express/Fastify) and Web Streams API (Next.js/Fetch), enabling deployment flexibility across Lambda, ECS, and serverless platforms.

title: Three-layer skill compatibility strategy
rationale: Native SKILL.md v2 format (backward-compatible with OpenClaw) for accessibility, MCP-first protocol for security/isolation, multi-platform adapters for ecosystem access. Enables 13,700+ OpenClaw + 200+ MCP + 30+ Strands tools while enforcing Cedar policies and sandboxing.

name: adapter-based-skill-compatibility
description: Thin adapter layer converts OpenClaw SKILL.md, Claude Code skills, MCP servers, and Strands @tool functions into unified ChimeraSkill interface without forking formats

title: MCP as universal tool protocol
rationale: 1,000+ MCP servers exist, cross-platform support (Claude, Cursor, AgentCore), enables tool reuse vs pure prompts

name: 5-tier-trust-model
description: Platform > Verified (Cedar policies) > Community (sandbox) > Private (tenant policies) > Experimental (strict isolation). Balances security with capability needs.

name: gsi2-tenant-lifecycle-queries
description: GSI2 on tenants table (status->tenantId) enables querying tenants by lifecycle state (PROVISIONING, ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, OFFBOARDING) for admin dashboards and tenant health monitoring

name: gsi2-user-session-tracking
description: GSI2 on sessions table (userId->lastActivity) enables per-user session queries for multi-device session management, user activity tracking, and concurrent session limits

content: Bun monorepo structure: root package.json with workspaces array, TypeScript project references with composite:true, shared types package (@chimera/shared) as foundation for all other packages

name: typescript-project-references-monorepo
description: Root tsconfig.json with empty files array and references to all packages. Each package extends root config and sets composite:true. Enables incremental builds and cross-package type checking

name: shared-types-package-foundation
description: @chimera/shared provides canonical TypeScript types mirroring DynamoDB schema. All packages reference shared to ensure type consistency. Types exported via barrel pattern (types/index.ts → src/index.ts)

name: api-gateway-rest-v1-waf-requirement
description: WAF WebACL association requires REST API v1 (not HTTP API v2). HTTP API is newer and 70% cheaper, but WAF WebACLAssociation only works with REST API regional endpoints. Use REST API when WAF is required, HTTP API for cost-optimized non-WAF endpoints.

name: api-gateway-jwt-tenant-isolation
description: API Gateway JWT authorizer with Cognito custom claims (custom:tenant_id) enables multi-tenant isolation without separate user pools. 5-minute cache balances auth performance vs tenant context freshness. Path parameter {tenantId} must match JWT claim for Cedar policy enforcement.

name: api-gateway-mock-integration-placeholder
description: Use MockIntegration returning 501 Not Implemented for API Gateway methods when Lambda functions don't exist yet. Avoids circular dependencies between ApiStack and PlatformRuntimeStack. Lambda integrations added later via updateMethod() without recreating the API.

name: typescript-monorepo-core-package
description: @chimera/core package follows monorepo patterns: workspace dependencies (@chimera/shared workspace:*), project references in tsconfig, barrel exports in src/index.ts. Build order: shared → core → other packages.

name: api-gateway-merge-conflict-resolution
description: When parallel stack development causes merge conflicts in chimera.ts, resolve by: (1) adding new stack file to lib/, (2) inserting stack instantiation with proper dependency chain, (3) updating tags loop to include new stack. ApiStack (Stack 5) depends on SecurityStack for Cognito/WAF, positioned after ObservabilityStack (Stack 4).

name: chat-stack-ecs-design
description: ChatStack uses Express/Fastify on ECS Fargate with @chimera/sse-bridge to translate Strands StreamEvents to Vercel DSP over SSE. ALB exposes /api/chat endpoint. Auto-scales 2-10 tasks (prod), 1-3 (dev). Platform adapter interface enables Slack/Web/Teams delivery.

name: skill-services-architecture
description: Production skill ecosystem services in packages/core/src/skills/ provide DDB-backed registry, installer with S3 bundle management, two-tier discovery (Bedrock KB + OpenSearch), 7-stage security validator, MCP Gateway client, and Cedar trust engine. Complements in-memory tools/ prototypes without replacing them.

content: @chimera/shared skill types follow SKILL.md v2 spec: SkillCategory (fixed taxonomy), SkillPermissions (filesystem/network/shell/memory/secrets), SkillDependencies (skills/mcp_servers/packages/binaries/env_vars), MCPServerConfig (transport/command/tools), SkillTestConfig, SecurityScanResult (7-stage pipeline), SkillBundle (S3 metadata), SkillInstall (tenant installations). Mirrors DynamoDB schema from canonical-data-model.md.

name: marketplace-skills-table-gsi-design
description: DynamoDB chimera-skills table for marketplace catalog uses PK=SKILL#{name}, SK=VERSION#{semver} with 3 GSIs: (1) GSI1-author (author->skillName) for creator portfolios, (2) GSI2-category (category->downloadCount) for trending/popular skills by category, (3) GSI3-trust (trustLevel->updatedAt) for browsing verified/recent additions. Numeric downloadCount as sort key enables efficient popularity queries without additional computation.

content: 8-stack CDK architecture complete: (1) Network, (2) Data, (3) Security, (4) Observability, (5) API Gateway, (6) Skill Pipeline, (7) Chat Gateway, (8) CI/CD Pipeline. PipelineStack is independent with no cross-stack dependencies, can be deployed separately. Uses context variables for flexibility: repository (owner/repo), branch (default: main), githubTokenSecret (Secrets Manager name).

name: tenant-billing-services
description: TenantService, QuotaManager, RateLimiter, CostTracker, and BudgetMonitor implement multi-tenant resource management with DynamoDB atomic operations, token bucket rate limiting, monthly cost aggregation with 2-year TTL, and budget enforcement with configurable alert thresholds

name: dynamodb-atomic-quota-consumption
description: QuotaManager and RateLimiter use DynamoDB ConditionExpression for atomic quota checks: UpdateItem with 'current + amount <= limit' prevents over-consumption in concurrent scenarios, with ConditionalCheckFailedException catching quota exhaustion

name: token-bucket-optimistic-locking
description: RateLimiter implements token bucket with optimistic locking: calculate refilled tokens based on elapsed time, then UpdateItem with ConditionExpression 'lastRefill = expected' to detect concurrent modifications, retrying on ConditionalCheckFailedException

name: Chimera CDK stack registry
description: After Phases 0-7: Network, Data, Security, Observability, Api, SkillPipeline, Chat, Evolution, Orchestration, Pipeline, TenantOnboarding. infra/bin/chimera.ts is the most conflict-prone file. Each phase adds stacks here.

content: Tech stack conventions: bun for all TypeScript tooling and runtime, mise (.mise.toml) for tool version management, Python for Lambda functions (boto3 access). Package names: @chimera/<name>. TypeScript strict mode, no default exports.

name: eventbridge-config-sync-pipeline
description: Real-time resource index sync: AWS Config change events → EventBridge rule → Lambda processor → DynamoDB resource index with GSI for tag queries. Lambda parses Config configuration items, extracts tags, queries Cost Explorer for resource costs (async), updates relationship graph. Enables <5min sync latency vs Config's native 5-15min aggregation lag. DynamoDB schema: PK=RESOURCE#arn, SK=METADATA; GSI1: PK=TAG#key:value for tag-based queries.

content: AWS account discovery triad: Config (comprehensive history + compliance), Resource Explorer (fast search <1s), CloudFormation (stack architecture + drift). Combined with Cost Explorer (spending) and Tags (universal taxonomy), these form complete account intelligence layer for agent systems.

aws-api-tool-prioritization: 25 core AWS services as agent tools across 4 tiers: Tier 1 (Core Compute & Storage): Lambda, EC2, ECS/Fargate, S3, CloudWatch. Tier 2 (Database & Messaging): DynamoDB, RDS, SQS, SNS, EventBridge. Tier 3 (Orchestration & ML): Step Functions, Bedrock, SageMaker. Tier 4 (Security & Advanced): IAM, Route 53, Cost Explorer. Implementation timeline: Q1=Tier 1, Q2=Tier 2, Q3=Tier 3, Q4=Tier 4.

name: bedrock-cross-region-inference-profiles
description: Bedrock cross-region inference profiles (us.anthropic.* model IDs) auto-route to nearest region, reducing latency 40-80ms. Same token pricing, no cross-region charges

name: cedar-provisioning-authorization
description: AWS Chimera uses Cedar policies to balance agent autonomy with safety. Seven policy categories: (1) cost-based (auto-approve <, tier limits), (2) change types (safe=env vars/scaling vs restricted=IAM/VPC), (3) resource types (whitelist ECS/Lambda, forbid IAM/VPC), (4) rate limits (max 5/hour), (5) emergency self-healing (critical alarms bypass approval), (6) regional compliance (GDPR data residency), (7) capability-based (data-pipeline vs ml-training agents). Result: 99.2% success rate, 5-minute average deployments.

name: progressive-refinement-lifecycle
description: Agent swarms implement 5-stage refinement: Discovery → POC → Prototype → Hardened → Production. Each stage has explicit quality gates, self-evaluation feedback loops, and autonomous blocker resolution. Critical decisions require human approval; minor decisions use reasonable defaults.

name: local-dev-mocks
description: Mock implementations (MockRuntime, MockMemoryClient, MockGatewayClient) simulate AgentCore services for local development. Pattern: implement production interfaces with in-memory storage (Map-based), enable seamless switching between local and production via dependency injection. Benefits: instant feedback loops, no AWS setup required, deterministic testing.

name: slack-discord-adapter-pattern
description: Platform adapters normalize webhook payloads into unified ChatMessage format and format responses with platform-specific rich content (Slack Block Kit 3000-char blocks, Discord embeds 2000-char limit)

name: aws-tool-client-factory
description: Cached SDK clients per region with tenant-scoped STS AssumeRole credentials. Client factory maintains Map with automatic TTL-based expiration (1hr credential cache) avoiding repeated AssumeRole calls while ensuring per-tenant IAM isolation.

name: aws-sdk-retry-backoff
description: Exponential backoff with jitter for AWS SDK retryable errors. Pattern: 3 retries max, baseDelay=1000ms, delay = baseDelay * 2^attempt + random(0-1000). Retryable errors: TooManyRequestsException, ThrottlingException, ServiceException, TimeoutError. Applied consistently across all 4 tools (Lambda, S3, EC2, CloudWatch).

name: discovery-service-complementarity
description: AWS discovery uses three complementary services: (1) Config provides comprehensive history + relationships (5-15min latency, SQL queries, 300+ resource types), (2) Resource Explorer enables instant tag-based search (<1s, wildcard queries, no setup), (3) CloudFormation tracks IaC provenance + drift detection. Each fills gaps in others' capabilities.

content: discovery-module-structure: Discovery module at packages/core/src/discovery/ contains: (1) types.ts with shared types for all 6 discovery services (ResourceMetadata, ConfigurationItem, ExplorerResource, StackSummary, DriftDetectionResult), (2) Service wrappers (config-scanner.ts, resource-explorer.ts, stack-inventory.ts) with stubs for AWS SDK integration, (3) Comprehensive type safety with DiscoveryError handling.

name: discovery-enrichment-layer
description: Cost Explorer (spending analysis) + Tag Organizer (governance) + Resource Index (in-memory aggregation) form enrichment layer above base discovery (Config, Resource Explorer, CloudFormation). ResourceIndex uses multiple secondary indexes (type, region, tag, stack) for O(1) lookups. CostAnalyzer with caching, TagOrganizer with CHIMERA_TAG_SCHEMA validation, ResourceIndex with Map-based indexes.

name: audit-trail-service-pattern
description: Comprehensive action logging service captures AWS API calls with full context: (1) ActionLog schema with 10+ nested structures (identity, classification, AWS API details, resource info, cost impact, execution context), (2) Three-tier storage (DynamoDB 0-90d hot, S3 1yr warm, Glacier 7yr cold), (3) Multiple query patterns (by tenant/time, resource ARN via GSI1, service type via GSI2, decision linkage), (4) Automatic tagging with chimera:* tags for resource tracking, (5) X-Ray trace ID generation for distributed tracing, (6) State change capture with JSONPatch diff for rollback capability

content: Activity logging generators follow cost-tracker class pattern: storage abstraction interface (ADRStorage, RunbookStorage), configuration-based initialization, pure rendering functions, and separate save/retrieve methods. Enables testing and multi-backend support (S3, DDB, git).

name: multi-account-organizations-client
description: ChimeraOrganizationsClient implements AWS Organizations operations (list accounts, create accounts, manage OUs) following AWSClientFactory pattern with cached clients, exponential backoff retry logic, and AWSToolResult wrapper for consistent error handling

name: multi-account-module-structure
description: Multi-account management module provides three complementary services: (1) SCPManager for org-level security guardrails (AWS Organizations), (2) BillingAggregator for consolidated cost allocation across linked accounts (Cost Explorer), (3) CrossAccountDiscovery for unified resource visibility (Config Aggregators + Resource Explorer). Each service follows factory pattern with config, client caching, error handling, and typed interfaces.

content: AWS Organizations API is global and only accessible from us-east-1 region. All OrganizationsClient instances must be created with region: 'us-east-1' regardless of tenant or default region settings. This differs from regional services like EC2, Lambda, S3.

well-architected-agent-decision-framework: Agents use AWS Well-Architected Framework's 6 pillars (Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, Sustainability) as structured decision vocabulary. Each infrastructure change is evaluated against all pillars with POSITIVE/NEUTRAL/NEGATIVE scores plus rationale.

name: well-architected-evaluation-structure
description: PillarEvaluation returns { pillar, score, severity?, rationale, metrics?, recommendations? } enabling both human explanation (rationale) and machine processing (score/severity). Three-tier recommendation system: APPROVE (no negatives), APPROVE_WITH_CAUTION (minor/moderate negatives), REJECT (major negatives).

name: well-architected-multi-format-presentation
description: Trade-off presentations support multiple output formats (markdown, Slack, email, API JSON) while maintaining consistent evaluation logic. Emoji indicators (✅ POSITIVE, ⚠️ NEGATIVE, ✔️ NEUTRAL) provide quick visual scanning across all formats.

name: well-architected-api-integration
description: AWS Well-Architected Tool API client for programmatic workload reviews: WellArchitectedToolAPI class with methods for CreateWorkload, UpdateAnswer, GetLensReview, CreateMilestone. Review automation via ReviewGenerator with infrastructure introspection (CloudFormation, IAM, DynamoDB) and six-pillar evaluation (Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance, Cost, Sustainability). Pattern: placeholder introspection methods allow incremental implementation without changing API surface.

name: index-additive-merge-pattern
description: When parallel builders add new modules, index.ts conflicts arise because each adds their export at EOF. Solution: additively combine all new exports rather than picking one version. Pattern applies to: (1) packages/core/src/index.ts for new top-level modules, (2) package-specific index.ts for new implementations within a module. Example: multi-account, infra-builder CDK/Cedar/Drift components. This preserves all parallel work and maintains barrel export consistency.

name: agentcore-strands-integration-hybrid-tenancy
description: Chimera AgentCore integration pattern: (1) Hybrid multi-tenancy: Basic/Advanced tiers share pooled AgentCore Runtime endpoint, Premium tier gets dedicated endpoints. (2) Memory namespace isolation: 'tenant-{tenant_id}-user-{user_id}' prevents cross-tenant leakage. (3) Cognito JWT claims → AgentCore session attributes for tenant context. (4) S3 workspace storage (not EFS) due to MicroVM ephemeral nature. (5) Gateway targets for skills: Lambda, MCP servers, OpenAPI, API Gateway, Smithy. (6) Tier-based memory strategies: Basic=SUMMARY, Advanced=SUMMARY+USER_PREFERENCE, Premium=SUMMARY+USER_PREFERENCE+SEMANTIC.

name: openclaw-tool-pipeline-pattern
description: OpenClaw tools go through 7-stage pipeline (base tools > custom replacements > platform tools > channel tools > policy filtering > schema normalization > abort wrapping) in src/agents/pi-tools.ts createOpenClawCodingTools. Chimera should replicate this pipeline pattern with AWS SDK tools and Cedar policy filtering.

name: openclaw-lazy-skill-loading
description: OpenClaw injects compact skill list (name, description, location) into system prompt. Model reads full SKILL.md only when needed. Keeps base prompt under 1000 tokens. Chimera should replicate this lazy-loading pattern for AWS-native skills.

name: openclaw-lane-queue-session-serialization
description: OpenClaw Lane Queue serializes agent runs per session (one turn at a time) to prevent tool/session races. Modes: collect, steer, followup. Critical reliability pattern for concurrent usage. Chimera needs DynamoDB-based equivalent for session locking.

name: strands-tool-conversion-pattern
description: Convert class-based AWS services to Strands tool format using factory functions. Pattern: (1) Create factory function createXTools(config) that returns tool array, (2) Each public method becomes standalone tool with tool({ name, description, inputSchema: z.object({...}), callback: async (input) => {...} }), (3) Private methods become module-level helper functions, (4) Input validation via Zod schemas with .describe() for docs, (5) Return JSON.stringify() for consistent output format. Dependencies: @strands-agents/sdk (tool decorator) and zod (schema validation). Converted 6 discovery services: config-scanner (6 tools), cost-analyzer (7 tools), resource-explorer (7 tools), stack-inventory (7 tools), tag-organizer (6 tools), resource-index (8 tools) = 41 total tools.

name: aws-tool-strands-factory-pattern
description: Convert class-based AWS tools to Strands tool() format using factory functions. Each service file exports create<Service>Tools(clientFactory) that returns Tool[]. Tenant context (tenantId, agentId, region) passed as explicit Zod input params. AWSClientFactory captured in closure. Shared retryWithBackoff extracted to tool-utils.ts. Returns JSON.stringify'd AWSToolResult. Binary params accepted as base64 strings.

title: TS agent uses Bedrock Converse API
rationale: TypeScript Strands SDK is experimental. ChimeraAgent uses @aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime ConverseStream API directly for local dev. Python strands-agents SDK (production-ready) used for AgentCore Runtime deployment.

content: agentcore-memory-tier-config: Memory configuration by tier mirrors Python agent pattern. Basic: SUMMARY only, STM window=10; Advanced: SUMMARY+USER_PREFERENCE, window=50; Premium: all strategies (SUMMARY+USER_PREFERENCE+SEMANTIC), window=200. Wired into ChimeraAgent constructor via getMemoryConfigForTier(). Memory client auto-initialized with tier config, stores messages on invoke().

ChimeraAgent skill loading pattern: AgentConfig.skills (string[]) + skillRegistry -> dynamic import of skill-bridge -> loadSkillsForAgent() -> injectSkillsIntoAgent(). Instruction skills inject into SystemPromptTemplate, MCP skills convert to loadedTools format {name, description, inputSchema, callback}. Uses dynamic import() to avoid circular dependency between agent and skills modules.

name: tenant-router-jwt-pattern
description: TenantRouter decodes Cognito JWT without verification (verification handled by API Gateway/Lambda authorizer), extracts tenantId from custom:tenantId attribute or TENANT# groups, loads full tenant config from DynamoDB, and builds TenantContext for downstream authorization. Routes dedicated tier tenants to dedicated clusters, shared tiers to multi-tenant clusters.

name: request-pipeline-composable-stages
description: RequestPipeline implements 4-stage composable pipeline: (1) Authentication (JWT decode + tenant lookup), (2) Authorization (Cedar policy evaluation), (3) Rate Limiting (token bucket + fail-open on error), (4) Quota Checking (resource limits + fail-closed on error). Each stage returns PipelineStageResult with allowed boolean + error details. Pipeline short-circuits on first deny. Optional stages skipped via skipRateLimiting/skipQuotaChecking flags.

content: tenant-router-jwt-ddb-pipeline: Multi-tenant request pipeline: (1) Cognito JWT extraction, (2) DynamoDB tenant config lookup, (3) Cedar cross-tenant DENY, (4) Token bucket rate limiting, (5) Quota enforcement. Pipeline short-circuits on first failure. Non-overlapping builder decomposition: core at packages/core/src/tenant/, gateway at packages/chat-gateway/.

name: observability-dashboard-specialization
description: Production monitoring uses 4 specialized CloudWatch dashboards: (1) Platform (API health, DDB throttles, ECS metrics, load test results) (2) TenantHealth (per-tenant session activity, request rates, error rates, latency p99) (3) SkillUsage (per-skill invocation counts, success/failure rates, execution latency) (4) CostAttribution (monthly spend per tenant, tier quota utilization, budget burn rate). Custom metrics published to Chimera/Tenant, Chimera/Skills, Chimera/Billing, Chimera/LoadTest namespaces.

content: production-observability-tiered-alarms: Severity-tiered SNS topics (Critical/PagerDuty, High/Slack, Medium/Email) with runbook URLs in alarm descriptions. 4 dashboards: Platform, TenantHealth, SkillUsage, CostAttribution. Custom metric namespaces: Chimera/Tenants, Chimera/Skills, Chimera/Billing, Chimera/LoadTest.

title: phase-6-evolution-integration-gates
rationale: Three critical integration points: (1) Bedrock API for prompt variant generation (Nova Micro meta-agent), (2) AgentCore sandbox gateway for prompt testing, (3) CDK diff generation for infrastructure proposals. These should be documented as patterns before builder implementation.

content: architecture-alignment-review-methodology: Vision vs implementation alignment review evaluates 12 operator vision points against actual code using 4-tier classification (Substantial 75%+, Partial 40-74%, Not Implemented 0%). Key findings: Evolution engine (80%) and Skill ecosystem (85%) are strongest. AWS service tools (16% - 4/25), Multi-modal (0%), and AgentCore CDK deployment are critical gaps. Review pattern: read vision docs, read implementation files in parallel, produce per-point verdict with specific file references.

aws-media-tools-pattern: Media service tools (Transcribe, Rekognition, Textract) follow Strands tool pattern: Zod input validation, tenant-scoped credentials via client factory, exponential backoff retry with service-specific error arrays (TRANSCRIBE_RETRYABLE_ERRORS, REKOGNITION_RETRYABLE_ERRORS, TEXTRACT_RETRYABLE_ERRORS), consistent JSON response format (success/data/metadata). Binary data handling: base64 encoding for inline input + S3 references (bucket/key/version) for large files. Operation patterns: sync operations for small/single-page content (Rekognition images, Textract single page), async job-based for large content (Transcribe audio/video, Textract multi-page PDFs) with job status polling.

media-processor-multimodal-pattern: MediaProcessor module provides auto-detection and routing for multi-modal inputs (audio/video → Transcribe, images → Rekognition, documents → Textract). Three-tier detection: explicit mediaType > MIME type > file extension. All AWS SDK clients injected via config following dependency injection pattern. S3 URIs required for Transcribe/Textract (no direct upload). Results structured with type discriminators for type-safe handling.

multimodal-media-processing-module: MediaProcessor at packages/core/src/media/ auto-detects input type (audio/video/image/document) from MIME type and file extension, routes to AWS Transcribe/Rekognition/Textract. Three new Strands tools at aws-tools/ follow existing s3-tool.ts pattern: createTranscribeTools (4 ops), createRekognitionTools (5 ops), createTextractTools (4 ops). Client factory extended with 3 new getters. Architecture doc Section 9 covers auto-routing pipeline, async processing pattern, cost tracking, Cedar policies.

aws-tool-factory-parallel-build: When adding many AWS tool files in parallel, the shared infrastructure (client-factory.ts, tool-utils.ts) must be COMPLETE before spawning tool builders. Incomplete infrastructure causes: (a) builders adding their own factory methods (FILE_SCOPE_VIOLATION, merge conflicts) or (b) placeholder files instead of implementations. Give the infra builder an exhaustive checklist of ALL services including their exact SDK client class names. Key naming gotcha: StepFunctions uses SFNClient (getSFNClient), not StepFunctionsClient.

name: chat-platform-user-resolution-middleware
description: Express middleware pattern for resolving chat platform users: (1) Extract platform user from request body (Slack event.user, Discord author.id, Teams from.id), (2) Query user-pairings table with platform+platformUserId, (3) Attach ResolvedUserContext to req.userContext, (4) Update lastActivityAt timestamp async. Middleware chain: extractTenantContext → resolveUser → route handler. Slack routes updated to use req.userContext.cognitoSub instead of raw event.user.

content: memory-scope-tiers: Three-tier memory scope architecture orthogonal to pricing tiers. SESSION scope (ephemeral, dies with session, maps to AgentCore STM), SWARM scope (shared namespace for multi-agent collaboration on same task, e.g., lead + builders on chimera-39d5), AGENT scope (persistent cross-session knowledge for specific agent, maps to AgentCore LTM). Namespace patterns: tenant-{tenantId}-user-{userId}-session-{sessionId}, tenant-{tenantId}-swarm-{swarmId}, tenant-{tenantId}-agent-{agentId}. TieredMemoryClient manages all three scopes with scope isolation, multi-scope retrieval, and independent cleanup.

tiered-memory-scope-architecture: Memory has two orthogonal axes: (1) SCOPE (SESSION/SWARM/AGENT) determines visibility and lifetime — who sees the data, (2) PRICING TIER (basic/advanced/premium) determines strategies — what processing is applied. TieredMemoryClient composes multiple MemoryClient instances via namespace isolation. Namespaces: tenant-{id}-user-{uid}-session-{sid} for SESSION, tenant-{id}-swarm-{taskId} for SWARM, tenant-{id}-agent-{agentId} for AGENT.

gateway-tier-access-pattern: Tool tier architecture separates AWS service tools (tiers 1-3 based on criticality) from discovery tools (special 'discovery' tier available to all subscription tiers). TenantTier maps to max tool tier: basic→1, advanced→2, enterprise/dedicated→3. Discovery tools bypass tier checks in isToolAvailable().

name: token-estimation-budget-gate
description: Pre-flight budget enforcement in agent invoke path: (1) estimateTokenCount uses text.length/4 heuristic (overestimates, safe for gating), (2) estimateRequestCost uses BEDROCK_MODELS cost table from evolution/types.ts, (3) BudgetMonitor.getBudgetAction returns allow/warn/block, (4) BudgetExceededError thrown on block with tenantId, estimatedCost, budgetRemaining. Non-blocking by default.

content: model-routing-toggleable-modes: Tenant model configuration includes routingMode: 'static' | 'auto'. Static mode uses defaultModel for all requests. Auto mode uses Thompson Sampling Bayesian routing. Mode is configurable per tenant without code changes.

tier-based-model-restrictions: TenantService.createTenant() enforces tier-based model pools: basic (2 models: Nova Lite, Sonnet), advanced (3 models: +Opus), enterprise/dedicated (4 models: +Nova Micro). Prevents lower tiers from accessing expensive models.

When Stack B depends on resources from Stack A (e.g., ChatStack using PipelineStack's ECR repository), Stack A must be created before Stack B in the CDK app, and Stack B must call addDependency(Stack A). This ensures proper CloudFormation dependency ordering and allows cross-stack references.

claude-code-channels-not-for-chimera-orchestration: Claude Code Channels (v2.1.80+) are MCP servers for pushing external events (Telegram/Discord/webhooks) into LOCAL Claude Code sessions. They are human-to-agent bridges, not agent-to-agent communication. Not suitable for Chimera production orchestration: local-only, session-bound, single-session, no multi-tenant, no IAM auth. Keep SQS FIFO + EventBridge for A2A. Claude Code Agent Teams (experimental) use JSONL file bus for local multi-session coordination -- also not a replacement for cloud-native primitives.

groupchat-extends-a2a-message: GroupChatMessage extends A2AMessage rather than standalone interface. Ensures compatibility with existing A2A routing and avoids type duplication. Adds groupId, visibility (group|all), and threadId fields.

vercel-ai-sdk-sse-transport: Vercel AI SDK (ai npm package / @ai-sdk/react) uses SSE over HTTP POST as default transport, not WebSocket. Data Stream Protocol (DSP) streams JSON objects as SSE events with x-vercel-ai-ui-message-stream: v1 header. Our ALB + CloudFront + ECS architecture is fully compatible with no changes needed. Custom transports (including WebSocket) are supported via ChatTransport interface but not required.

async-message-completion-pattern: Vercel AI SDK provides built-in async completion via three composable primitives: (1) consumeStream() decouples LLM stream from HTTP response so agent finishes even after client disconnect, (2) consumeSseStream callback tees the SSE stream for server-side buffering, (3) useChat resume:true auto-reconnects via GET /api/chat/{id}/stream. onFinish callback receives isDisconnect flag. For Chimera: use DynamoDB-based stream buffering (no Redis needed), extend chimera-sessions with messages/activeStreamId/streamBuffer/status fields, hybrid in-memory + DDB approach for reconnection.

manus-ai-capabilities-mapping: Manus AI is a proprietary autonomous agent platform (CodeAct execution model) with browser automation, OAuth connectors, scheduled tasks, webhooks, webapp builder, session replay. Chimera has AWS-native equivalents for ~70%. Key gaps to fill: SES email, OAuth connector framework, EventBridge scheduled agent tasks, webhook lifecycle delivery, visual session replay.

stream-buffer-hybrid-ddb: StreamBufferService uses hybrid in-memory + DDB approach for async completion: chunks accumulate in Map<streamId, chunks> during streaming (zero DDB writes per token), then flushed atomically on completeStream(). Stream records stored in chimera-sessions table at SK=STREAM#{streamId}. TTL defaults to 1h after completion for reconnection window.

name: consumeStream-disconnect-resilience
description: POST /chat/stream registers stream with AsyncStreamManager before returning HTTP Response. StreamTee.consume() runs in background Promise, continues buffering even after client disconnect. ReadableStream.cancel() clears keepalive timer only — tee is unaffected. GET /chat/stream/:id replays buffer + follows live tail. JS single-threaded event loop makes register-then-replay race-free (no await between addListener and buffer replay).

content: async-message-completion-pattern: Chat gateway uses StreamTee pub/sub in sse-bridge for fan-out to multiple consumers. DDB StreamBufferService in packages/core buffers messages during disconnect. GET /api/messages/:sessionId/consume endpoint for reconnection with Last-Event-ID resume. Keepalive pings prevent proxy timeouts.

tenant-lifecycle-status-provisioning: TenantStatus includes PROVISIONING and DEPROVISIONED for lifecycle state tracking. changeTier() transitions: ACTIVE → PROVISIONING → ACTIVE (with new tier). DEPROVISIONED is reserved for offboarding.

tier-change-atomic-ddb-pattern: TenantLifecycleService.changeTier() updates CONFIG#features, CONFIG#models, and QUOTA#* limits concurrently via Promise.all(), bookended by two PROFILE updates (PROVISIONING then ACTIVE). Quota limits are keyed as agent-sessions, api-requests, tokens-monthly and only the limit field is updated (current usage preserved).

tenant-offboarding-sfn-no-compensation: Offboarding SFN routes errors to Fail state (not compensation). Each cleanup step is idempotent so re-execution is the recovery path. Contrast with onboarding which uses saga rollback.

tenant-offboarding-ddb-tombstone: finalizeTenantOffboarding uses PutItem (upsert) to write a sparse CHURNED PROFILE record after cleanupDdbItems deletes all tenant items. Preserves audit trail as a tombstone record.

tier-naming-canonical: TenantTier is 'basic|advanced|premium' (not enterprise/dedicated). Dedicated deployment is deploymentModel: 'shared'|'dedicated' on TenantProfile. Implemented in ADR-024 gap fix (chimera-1af1).

name: gateway-tool-migration-pattern
description: Migrate agent-embedded tools to Gateway targets by: (1) Python GatewayToolDiscovery class with lazy importlib imports and tier-based filtering mirroring TypeScript tier-config.ts, (2) CDK GatewayRegistrationStack with 4 Lambda targets per tier group using ChimeraLambda + SSM params for runtime ARN discovery, (3) Refactoring chimera_agent.py from 90+ hardcoded imports to single discover_tools() call. Backward compat via local fallback when Gateway unavailable.
